content.full_text:
SCHOOLS LESAL SERVICE
FRANK J. FEKETE
GENEBAL COUNSEL
PETER C. CAFITON
STEPHEN L. HARTSELL
D\/VAINE L. CHAMBERS
BOGEF R, GRASS
FRANK W, RONICH
MELVIN D. PETERS
COUNSEL
CARL B.A. LANGE III
DIRECTOR OF LABOFI RELATIONS
PHIL LANCASTEB
ANTHONY V, LEONIS
BARGAINING SPECIALIST
[re i-
- rr _ l
MAILING ADDRESS: P.O. BOx t tllO
5AO1 SUNDALE AVENUE
BAKERSFIELD, CALIFORNIA 93349
(aos) 3ga-3a3o
FAX 39a3843
February 22, L990
Kathy Donovan, Chief Counsel
Fair Po1itical Practi,ces Commission
Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Re: Request for Opinion; Elected official 
Asserting
A Dual Role as Trustee and Employee
Dear Ms. Donovan:
We represent the San Luis coastal Unified Schoo1 District. 
Board
member caroLine Botwin (rtBotvrin"), a newly-elected trustee, rrants
to serve the district 
both on the governing board and as a paid
employee (teacher).
The board has asked us to investigate the obvious conflict of
interest issues raised by this dual--role status. 
we are working
with the office of the Attorney ceneral on issues presented by
Government Code Section 1090, et seq., Government Code Section
1126, and the connon 1a!, of conflicts of interest and incompatible
activities. 
At the same time, v/e are contacting your office with
respect to conflict issues arising under the Political Reforn Act
of 1974 (Goverrunent Code Sections 8L000, et seq.), which we call
the trActtr in this letter,
We talked with one of your staff attorneys last week to get sone
general procedural information. 
Beyond confirming some general
observations about how the Act and your Agency work, she suggested
that we put the district's 
questions in writing. 
We understand
that your Agency responds to opinion requests such as ours within
25 days.
The questions arise out of the foLlowing fact situation.
FACTS PRESENTED
A district ernployee, Botwin, is elected to the governing board at
the Novenber 1989 election. Upon assuning the public office of
trustee, Botwin declines to resign the enployment ro1e. Instead,
Bot!'rin asserts a right to remain a paid employee while
sinultaneously sitting as a trustee.




Kathy Donovan, chief counsel
February 22, l99O
Page 2
Botwin is a mernber of a the districtrs 
certificated 
employee
collective bargaining unit. 
The barqaining agreernent between the
district governing board and the unit covers many topics, including
discipline, reassignment, layoff and compensation of unit rnembers.
At the same time, board menbers -- including Botwin -- would
regularly be asked to consider agenda iterns invol,ving natters such
as disciptine, reass j-gnment, Iayoff and compensaiion of Eoiiiin aird
other unit mernbers. Each budget augmentation and expenditure iten
and decision necessarity affects the amount of money available for
compensation of employees, as determined by collective bargaining.
Each board agenda will include matters in which Botwin has an
interest, including matters such as -
1. 
Nonreelection under Education code section 44929.2L of
certificated enployees.
2. 
compensation for classified staff 
(in the bargaining
unit ) .
3. 
Purchase of a portable building (as a representative
purchaser of goods and services).
4. Monthly certificated salary warrant approval for Botwin
and for other employees under Education code Section 45055.
5. Consulting with and instructing the board's negotiator as
to changes to the collective bargaining agreements for certificated
and classified staff.
PRELIMINARY OBSERVATIONS
Your staff attorney agreed with us about certain prelininary
observations.
1. The Act does not allow your Aqency to review or pass on
conflict of interest issues arising under Government Code Sections
1090, et seq., Government Code section 1126 and the common law of
conflicts of interest and incompatible activities. 
You routinely
refer those natters back to the public entityrs counsel or suggest
a consultation with the Attorney General's office.
2. Public entity ernployment earningrs are apparently exenpt
from the reporting portion of the Actrs prohibitions against having


Kathy Donovan, Chief Counsel
February 22, l99o
Page 3
a financial interest. 
Government code Section 82030, subdivision
(b) (2), definitionally 
exempts public entity income from the
financial interest disclosure reguirements of covernment code
sections 87100 and 87Lo3. But the Act do6E rot oxenpt an
officialrs 
interest 
in a busj-ness entity 
from the basic
prohibitions against conflict of interest in Sections 87100, et
seq'
3. while the district 
is apparently not a business entity
under section 82005, you have never opined as to whether an
employee union or bargaining unit association is a business entity
within that definition.
4. Those persons drafting the Title 2 regulations apparently
assurned that no elected board nenber night ever assert a right to
serve in a dual capacity sirnultaneously as an employee and an
employee of the sarne public entity. 
Actually, that reconfirms the
boardrs perception of what has always been the law in this state.
A cursory examination of the history of similar posts, such as
county supervisor or city council member, confirms the statewide
contemporaneous administrative construction that a dual capacity
situation is unlawful and inpossible.
QUESTIONS PRESENTED
With the above in mind, the foltowing are the district's 
initial
questions under the Actts conflict of interest sections (Government
Code Sections 87100-87313) .
1. Is Botwin's certificated bargaining unit a business entity
within the meaning of Government code section 82005?
2. Please teII us the extent, if any, to which the following
are activities 
from which Botwin is disqualified under the Act:
A. Participating in board deliberations or voting on
whether to give an Education code Section 44929.21 notice of
nonreelection to a fellow certificated unit member.
B. earticipating in board deliberations or voting on
whether to irnplement incentive pay or any other changes in
compensation for classified staff.
c. Participating in board deliberations or voting on a
proposal to expend district funds for a portable building or
other goods and services).


Kathy Donovan, chief counsel
February 22, L99O
Page 4
D. Participating in board deliberations or voting under
Education code section 45055 on vrhether to approve salary
warrants for Bot$rin or for others in the bargaining unit.
E. 
Participating in or voting on any matter arising
under or affecting collective bargaining for any employee
gror:n or rrnit.
OUR PRELIMINARY CONCLUSIONS
we have conducted a prelirninary analysis of the Act and the
subsequently-adopted Title 
8 regulations. 
Here are our
observations and conclusions:
1. 
Botwin's certificated bargaining unit is a recognj,zed
exclusive representative under the Educational Employnent Relations
Act (covernment code Sections 3540, et seq. ) As such, the unit is
a business entity within the rneaning of Government code Section
87103, subdivisions (a) and (d) and Title 2 regulation section
18700.1, subdivision (a). At a ninimum, Botwin's relationship and
menbership in the unit reguires (a) reporting the existence of the
interest and (b) [under Title 
2 regu].ation Section ]-8702.1,
subdivision (a) (4) I disqualification on a1l matters relating to
Botwints unit.
2. When voting, Botwin 'rmakes a governnental decisiontr under
covernment Code Section 87100. This seerns confirmed by Title 
2
regulation Section 18700, subdivision (b) (1).
3. When joininq in board deLiberations, Botwj.n participates
in the rrrnaking of a governmental decj.sion" under Government Code
Sectj-on 87100. This seerns confirrned by Title 2 regulation Section
18700, subdivision (c).
4. 
To the extent that Title 2 regulation Section 18700
subdivision (d) night appear to exenpt a board member from the
Actrs proscriptions against decision-rnaking or participation in
decisions on Botwin's relationship to the bargaining unit, the
regulation appears to be arnbiguous and in conflict with the purpose
of the statute and requires clarification 
when applied to these
facts. This regulatory exenptj-on does not appear to have addressed
- or been intended to exempt - a board menberrs action on his or
her osrn barqaini,ng unit contract.


Kathy Donovan, Chief Counsel
February 22, L990
Paqe 5
5. 
Similarly, the apparent exenption language in section
Title 2 regulation Section 18700.1, subdivision (b)(3), absolving
attenpts to influence others when an official 
'rnegotiates his or
her compensation or the terms and conditions of his or her
employment or contractrr appears to conflict with the purpose of the
statute and seems unlalrful as applied to these facts. 
This
exemption does not appear to have addressed - or been intended to
exernpt - a board mernberrs actj.on on his or her own bargaining uni.t
contract .
6. 
Each of the board decisions referred to in the typical
agenda items above requires the exercise of discretion rather than
being soIeIy ttrninisterial, secretariat, manual or clericalrt under
regulation Section 18700, subdivision (d) (1).
7. 
Botwinrs interest in each transaction by the governing
board affecting her bargaining unit disqualifies Botwin from
participation or voting under penalty of civil 
and/or crininal
proceedings under covernnent Code Sections 91OOO, et seq. We nust
therefore conclude that 
the Act disqualifies 
Botwin from
participating or votin!, on any of the agenda items listed above.
And if Botwin attenpts to vote, that vote must not be considered
or counted.
A CONCLUDING NOTE
Even if your Agency rnight disagree with one or more of our
preliminary conclusions as to conflicts arising under the Act, your
opinion will 
not address the separate conflict 
of interest
prohibitions under covernnent Code Sections 1090, et seq.,
Government Code Section 1,1?5 and the common law of conflicts of
interest and incompatible aci:ivities. 
For instance, the Act under
which your Agency operates does not provide for invalidation of a
entire board vote to approve a matter in which Botwin has a Section
1090 interest. 
covernment Code Sections 1090, et seq., on the
other hand, provide additional authority to void the entire
transaction. 
See Thomson v. CaII (1985) 38 Cal 3d 633, 2L4 Cat.
Rptr. 139.
Your reply should be directed to this office. 
please ca1l if you
need any additional infornation.
PCC : cp
